title: Troubleshooting Playbook: Settlement Variance
neverClick: row links / table rows (browse-and-point only; this playbook opens no detail rows)

preflight.note: No persisted-preference staging is forced for this lesson. The start page (payments-reconciliation.html) is an orphan/standalone page in the demo: it has no owning module (the rail highlights 'Academy' and the left sidebar shows whatever module context was last active — IT & Support/Academy/Marketing across probes), it is not indexed in the command palette ('No results for reconciliation'), and no in-app link navigates to it. It is reachable only by its direct URL, which is this lesson's startUrl (probe-verified July 11). Consequences handled in the timeline: (1) All ledger targets are addressed by live JS/text selectors and log-rect geometry, so the reconciliation sidebar being collapsed OR expanded is immaterial to callout/highlight anchoring — every target resolved to exactly one visible element in both states. The camera framings for steps 1-5 favour the money columns and the right-hand controls (Expected/Settled/Variance, Review chip, Resolve batch, Search/Filters), which are right-edge-anchored and move < 75px between sidebar states, so framing holds regardless. Forcing the shared sidebar toggle (washcentral-sidebar-collapsed-v5) was deliberately avoided: it is a view preference shared across the profile and offers no functional gain here. (2) Because no genuine on-screen control navigates back to the orphan ledger, the step-11 recap is delivered over the Payouts page (the shot list already permits step 10 to hold on Payouts) rather than faking a nonexistent 'back to Payments Reconciliation' control — see RESIDUAL R4 in the build report.
